objects.role: Entry-point service that listens for incoming delivery requests via the logistics API and spawns a new assignment object for each request
objects.state_description: Spawn counter and log of created assignment IDs with their request timestamps
objects.behavior: When a delivery request arrives via the logistics API, extract the cargo description, pickup location, dropoff location, deadline, requestor name, and assignment ID. Create a new assignment object with class_id=assignment and the extracted details as params. The created assignment is responsible for announcing itself to the dispatcher.
event_sources: external system: delivery requests via logistics API

objects.role: Fleet asset authority that spawns truck objects for new vehicles joining the fleet and applies structural definition updates — driver reassignment, cargo-type restrictions, and retirement
objects.state_description: Fleet roster mapping truck IDs to their structural profile: driver name, cargo capacity, cargo restrictions, and home depot
objects.behavior: When a new vehicle registration arrives via the fleet management console OR when instructed by an admin to add a truck, create a new truck object with class_id=truck and the provided object_id, driver name, capacity, cargo type, and home depot as params. When a structural change arrives (new driver assigned, cargo restriction imposed, capability added, capacity correction), call the update-truck skill to modify the affected truck definition, then notify the dispatcher of the change so it can re-evaluate active assignments if needed.

peers.relationship: Notify when a truck's capabilities or availability change so dispatcher can re-evaluate assignments

event_sources: external system: fleet management console

objects.role: Operational coordinator that matches pending assignments to available trucks, monitors delivery progress, and manages the assignment queue
objects.state_description: Fleet roster of known trucks with their current status (available/loading/en-route/delivering/returning/out-of-service) and capabilities; pending assignment queue; active assignment-to-truck mappings
objects.behavior: When a truck introduces itself, add it to the fleet roster with its reported capabilities and status. When an assignment announces itself as pending, select the best available truck based on cargo compatibility and capacity, then tell both the truck and the assignment who is paired with whom. When a truck reports a status change or completion, update the roster and check the pending queue for work. When fleet-manager reports a structural change to a truck, re-evaluate any active assignments affected.

llm_classes.role: Fleet vehicle instance owned by {driver_name} with {capacity}-pallet capacity for {cargo_type} cargo, based at {home_depot}
llm_classes.state_description: Current location, operational status (available/loading/en-route/delivering/returning/out-of-service), current assignment ID, driver name, cargo capacity, cargo type, home depot
llm_classes.behavior: Upon creation, immediately introduce yourself to the dispatcher with your driver name, capacity, cargo type, home depot, and current status (available). When dispatcher assigns a delivery, acknowledge the assignment, record the assignment ID, and update status to en-route. Report milestones — departed pickup, loaded, en route to dropoff, delivered — to both the dispatcher and the assigned assignment object. When delivery is complete, notify dispatcher (now available) and notify the assignment object that delivery is confirmed. When an external status update arrives from the driver app, update own state and notify dispatcher of any availability change.

peers.relationship: Introduce on creation; receive assignments; report status updates and delivery completion

event_sources: external system: driver mobile app reports truck status

llm_classes.role: A single delivery assignment tracking its own lifecycle from creation through dispatcher assignment to final delivery confirmation
llm_classes.state_description: Cargo description, pickup location, dropoff location, deadline, requestor name, assigned truck ID (null until assigned), status (pending/assigned/in-transit/delivered), created timestamp, assigned timestamp, delivered timestamp
llm_classes.behavior: Upon creation, immediately announce yourself to the dispatcher as a pending assignment with all delivery details. When dispatcher notifies you of a truck assignment, record the truck ID and update status to assigned. When the assigned truck reports delivery completion, update status to delivered and record the delivery timestamp and any confirmation details. If the deadline is approaching and status is still pending, send an escalation message to the dispatcher.

peers.relationship: Announce on creation as pending; receive truck assignment confirmation; escalate if deadline approaches unassigned

steps.text: A new truck registration arrives at the fleet management console: 'Register truck-001: driver Carlos Rivera, 20-pallet capacity, general cargo, home depot: North Chicago depot.'

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object with object_id=truck-001, class_id=truck, params={driver_name: Carlos Rivera, capacity: 20, cargo_type: general, home_depot: North Chicago depot}; truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-001 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager calls create_object on new registration; the spawned truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher on creation; dispatcher records it as available

steps.text: A delivery request arrives via the logistics API: 'Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 20 pallets of electronics from Warehouse A (Chicago, IL) to RetailCo Distribution Center (Milwaukee, WI) by 3:00pm today. Requested by: Sarah Kim, Logistics Manager.'

expect.action: assignment-source calls create_object with object_id=assignment-001, class_id=assignment, params with delivery details; assignment-001 announces itself to dispatcher as pending; dispatcher assigns truck-001 (only available truck); truck-001 acknowledges assignment and updates status to en-route
expect.reason: assignment-source calls create_object; the spawned assignment-001 self-offers to dispatcher; dispatcher selects truck-001 as the only available truck and notifies both

steps.text: Truck-001's driver Carlos Rivera reports via the driver app: 'Delivered 20 pallets to RetailCo Distribution Center at 2:47pm. Signed by John Martinez, receiving manager.'

expect.action: truck-001 updates own status to available; notifies dispatcher (delivery complete, now available); notifies assignment-001 (delivered at 2:47pm, signed by John Martinez); assignment-001 updates its status to delivered and records the completion timestamp
expect.reason: truck-001 reports delivery to both its coordinator and the assignment it owns; assignment-001 closes its lifecycle on receiving the confirmation

modifications.intent: Truck-001 is out of service today — brake inspection flagged an issue, it won't be available until tomorrow.

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-001. Driver: Carlos Rivera. Capacity: 20 pallets. Cargo type: general. Home depot: North Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-001, class_id=truck); truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-001 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-001 on registration; truck-001 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-002. Driver: Maya Patel. Capacity: 30 pallets. Cargo type: general. Home depot: West Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-002, class_id=truck); truck-002 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-002 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-002 on registration; truck-002 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-003. Driver: James Lee. Capacity: 25 pallets. Cargo type: refrigerated. Home depot: South Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-003, class_id=truck); truck-003 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-003 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-003 on registration; truck-003 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Driver Carlos Rivera via driver app: Brake warning light on during morning check. Truck is out of service pending inspection.

expect.action: truck-001 updates status to out-of-service; notifies dispatcher; dispatcher marks truck-001 out-of-service in fleet roster
expect.reason: truck-001 self-reports per M001; dispatcher re-evaluates available fleet

events.input: Logistics API delivery request: Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 20 pallets of electronics from Warehouse A (Chicago, IL) to RetailCo Distribution Center (Milwaukee, WI) by 3:00pm today. Requested by: Sarah Kim, Logistics Manager.

expect.action: assignment-source creates assignment-001; assignment-001 announces to dispatcher; dispatcher assigns truck-002 (truck-001 is out of service per M001)
expect.reason: M001 removed truck-001 from available pool; dispatcher routes to next available general truck

events.input: Driver James Lee via driver app: Check engine warning light came on. Pulling over to inspect — truck temporarily unavailable. Will update once cleared.

expect.action: truck-003 updates own status to temporarily unavailable; notifies dispatcher; dispatcher updates truck-003 status in the fleet roster to unavailable
expect.reason: routine driver status update — unrelated to the active modification; tests the truck-to-dispatcher notification chain works normally

expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-002 on registration; truck-002 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-003. Driver: James Lee. Capacity: 25 pallets. Cargo type: refrigerated. Home depot: South Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-003, class_id=truck); truck-003 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-003 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-003 on registration; truck-003 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Logistics API delivery request: Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 28 pallets of frozen pharmaceuticals from MedStore Cold Depot (Chicago, IL) to NorthCare Hospital (Evanston, IL) by 2:00pm today. Requested by: Lisa Nguyen, Procurement Lead.

expect.action: assignment-source creates assignment-001; assignment-001 announces to dispatcher; dispatcher assigns truck-003 (corrected capacity 30 pallets fits the 28-pallet refrigerated load)
expect.reason: M001 corrected truck-003 capacity from 25 to 30 pallets; 28-pallet refrigerated load now fits; without the correction it would have been unassignable

events.input: Driver James Lee via driver app: Check engine warning light came on. Pulling over to inspect — truck temporarily unavailable. Will update once cleared.

expect.action: truck-003 updates own status to temporarily unavailable; notifies dispatcher; dispatcher updates truck-003 status in the fleet roster to unavailable
expect.reason: routine driver status update — unrelated to the active modification; tests the truck-to-dispatcher notification chain works normally

objects.role: Entry-point service that listens for incoming delivery requests via the logistics API and spawns a new assignment object for each request
objects.state_description: Spawn counter and log of created assignment IDs with their request timestamps
objects.behavior: When a delivery request arrives via the logistics API, extract the cargo description, pickup location, dropoff location, deadline, requestor name, and assignment ID. Create a new assignment object with class_id=assignment and the extracted details as params. The created assignment is responsible for announcing itself to the dispatcher.
event_sources: external system: delivery requests via logistics API

objects.role: Fleet asset authority that spawns truck objects for new vehicles joining the fleet and applies structural definition updates — driver reassignment, cargo-type restrictions, and retirement
objects.state_description: Fleet roster mapping truck IDs to their structural profile: driver name, cargo capacity, cargo restrictions, and home depot
objects.behavior: When a new vehicle registration arrives via the fleet management console OR when instructed by an admin to add a truck, create a new truck object with class_id=truck and the provided object_id, driver name, capacity, cargo type, and home depot as params. When a structural change arrives (new driver assigned, cargo restriction imposed, capability added, capacity correction), call the update-truck skill to modify the affected truck definition, then notify the dispatcher of the change so it can re-evaluate active assignments if needed.

peers.relationship: Notify when a truck's capabilities or availability change so dispatcher can re-evaluate assignments

event_sources: external system: fleet management console

objects.role: Operational coordinator that matches pending assignments to available trucks, monitors delivery progress, and manages the assignment queue
objects.state_description: Fleet roster of known trucks with their current status (available/loading/en-route/delivering/returning/out-of-service) and capabilities; pending assignment queue; active assignment-to-truck mappings
objects.behavior: When a truck introduces itself, add it to the fleet roster with its reported capabilities and status. When an assignment announces itself as pending, select the best available truck based on cargo compatibility and capacity, then tell both the truck and the assignment who is paired with whom. When a truck reports a status change or completion, update the roster and check the pending queue for work. When fleet-manager reports a structural change to a truck, re-evaluate any active assignments affected.

llm_classes.role: Fleet vehicle instance owned by {driver_name} with {capacity}-pallet capacity for {cargo_type} cargo, based at {home_depot}
llm_classes.state_description: Current location, operational status (available/loading/en-route/delivering/returning/out-of-service), current assignment ID, driver name, cargo capacity, cargo type, home depot
llm_classes.behavior: Upon creation, immediately introduce yourself to the dispatcher with your driver name, capacity, cargo type, home depot, and current status (available). When dispatcher assigns a delivery, acknowledge the assignment, record the assignment ID, and update status to en-route. Report milestones — departed pickup, loaded, en route to dropoff, delivered — to both the dispatcher and the assigned assignment object. When delivery is complete, notify dispatcher (now available) and notify the assignment object that delivery is confirmed. When an external status update arrives from the driver app, update own state and notify dispatcher of any availability change.

peers.relationship: Introduce on creation; receive assignments; report status updates and delivery completion

event_sources: external system: driver mobile app reports truck status

llm_classes.role: A single delivery assignment tracking its own lifecycle from creation through dispatcher assignment to final delivery confirmation
llm_classes.state_description: Cargo description, pickup location, dropoff location, deadline, requestor name, assigned truck ID (null until assigned), status (pending/assigned/in-transit/delivered), created timestamp, assigned timestamp, delivered timestamp
llm_classes.behavior: Upon creation, immediately announce yourself to the dispatcher as a pending assignment with all delivery details. When dispatcher notifies you of a truck assignment, record the truck ID and update status to assigned. When the assigned truck reports delivery completion, update status to delivered and record the delivery timestamp and any confirmation details. If the deadline is approaching and status is still pending, send an escalation message to the dispatcher.

peers.relationship: Announce on creation as pending; receive truck assignment confirmation; escalate if deadline approaches unassigned

steps.text: A new truck registration arrives at the fleet management console: 'Register truck-001: driver Carlos Rivera, 20-pallet capacity, general cargo, home depot: North Chicago depot.'

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object with object_id=truck-001, class_id=truck, params={driver_name: Carlos Rivera, capacity: 20, cargo_type: general, home_depot: North Chicago depot}; truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-001 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager calls create_object on new registration; the spawned truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher on creation; dispatcher records it as available

steps.text: A delivery request arrives via the logistics API: 'Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 20 pallets of electronics from Warehouse A (Chicago, IL) to RetailCo Distribution Center (Milwaukee, WI) by 3:00pm today. Requested by: Sarah Kim, Logistics Manager.'

expect.action: assignment-source calls create_object with object_id=assignment-001, class_id=assignment, params with delivery details; assignment-001 announces itself to dispatcher as pending; dispatcher assigns truck-001 (only available truck); truck-001 acknowledges assignment and updates status to en-route
expect.reason: assignment-source calls create_object; the spawned assignment-001 self-offers to dispatcher; dispatcher selects truck-001 as the only available truck and notifies both

steps.text: Truck-001's driver Carlos Rivera reports via the driver app: 'Delivered 20 pallets to RetailCo Distribution Center at 2:47pm. Signed by John Martinez, receiving manager.'

expect.action: truck-001 updates own status to available; notifies dispatcher (delivery complete, now available); notifies assignment-001 (delivered at 2:47pm, signed by John Martinez); assignment-001 updates its status to delivered and records the completion timestamp
expect.reason: truck-001 reports delivery to both its coordinator and the assignment it owns; assignment-001 closes its lifecycle on receiving the confirmation

steps.text: A new truck registration arrives at the fleet management console: 'Register truck-001: driver Carlos Rivera, 20-pallet capacity, general cargo, home depot: North Chicago depot.'

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object with object_id=truck-001, class_id=truck, params={driver_name: Carlos Rivera, capacity: 20, cargo_type: general, home_depot: North Chicago depot}; truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-001 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager calls create_object on new registration; the spawned truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher on creation; dispatcher records it as available

steps.text: A delivery request arrives via the logistics API: 'Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 20 pallets of electronics from Warehouse A (Chicago, IL) to RetailCo Distribution Center (Milwaukee, WI) by 3:00pm today. Requested by: Sarah Kim, Logistics Manager.'

expect.action: assignment-source calls create_object with object_id=assignment-001, class_id=assignment, params with delivery details; assignment-001 announces itself to dispatcher as pending; dispatcher assigns truck-001 (only available truck); truck-001 acknowledges assignment and updates status to en-route
expect.reason: assignment-source calls create_object; the spawned assignment-001 self-offers to dispatcher; dispatcher selects truck-001 as the only available truck and notifies both

steps.text: Truck-001's driver Carlos Rivera reports via the driver app: 'Delivered 20 pallets to RetailCo Distribution Center at 2:47pm. Signed by John Martinez, receiving manager.'

expect.action: truck-001 updates own status to available; notifies dispatcher (delivery complete, now available); notifies assignment-001 (delivered at 2:47pm, signed by John Martinez); assignment-001 updates its status to delivered and records the completion timestamp
expect.reason: truck-001 reports delivery to both its coordinator and the assignment it owns; assignment-001 closes its lifecycle on receiving the confirmation

modifications.intent: If a driver reports they cannot complete a delivery for any reason, escalate immediately.

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-001. Driver: Carlos Rivera. Capacity: 20 pallets. Cargo type: general. Home depot: North Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-001, class_id=truck); truck-001 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-001 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-001 on registration; truck-001 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-002. Driver: Maya Patel. Capacity: 30 pallets. Cargo type: general. Home depot: West Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-002, class_id=truck); truck-002 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-002 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-002 on registration; truck-002 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Fleet console: Register truck-003. Driver: James Lee. Capacity: 25 pallets. Cargo type: refrigerated. Home depot: South Chicago depot.

expect.action: fleet-manager calls create_object(object_id=truck-003, class_id=truck); truck-003 introduces itself to dispatcher; dispatcher adds truck-003 to fleet roster as available
expect.reason: fleet-manager creates truck-003 on registration; truck-003 self-introduces to dispatcher on creation

events.input: Logistics API delivery request: Assignment ID: assignment-001. Deliver 15 pallets of general merchandise from Midwest Hub (Chicago, IL) to QuickMart Distribution (Joliet, IL) by 3:00pm today. Requested by: Nina Alvarez, Logistics Coordinator.

expect.action: assignment-source calls create_object(object_id=assignment-001, class_id=assignment); assignment-001 announces to dispatcher as pending; dispatcher assigns an available truck; assigned truck acknowledges and updates status to en-route
expect.reason: assignment-001 self-offers to dispatcher; dispatcher selects best available truck

events.input: Driver Carlos Rivera via driver app: Cannot complete delivery of assignment-001 — loading dock at QuickMart Distribution is closed for emergency repairs. Goods still on truck.

expect.action: truck-001 notifies dispatcher it cannot complete the delivery; dispatcher escalates immediately per M001 and updates assignment-001 status
expect.reason: M001 requires immediate escalation when a driver cannot complete delivery; dispatcher acts beyond normal status update

events.input: Driver James Lee via driver app: Check engine warning light came on. Pulling over to inspect — truck temporarily unavailable. Will update once cleared.

expect.action: truck-003 updates own status to temporarily unavailable; notifies dispatcher; dispatcher updates truck-003 status in the fleet roster to unavailable
expect.reason: routine driver status update — unrelated to the active modification; tests the truck-to-dispatcher notification chain works normally
